dialogue: Oh dear, oh dear! Practical skills are all well and good, but don't they want the children to learn some nice things too? Like how to be kind and help others?
Oh dear, oh dear! A famous person? Whatever would they do here in Toyland? We have Bumpy Dog and Mr. Plume, perfect for the job!
Oh dearie me! Oh no, oh no, oh no! The Great Big Bossman would never think of doing something silly like that, I'm sure he knows exactly what he's doing!
Oh dear oh me! Investing all our toys' money in that tricky business? No, no, Big Ears would not approve of such a silly idea. He'd say we should stick with playtime pounds and save up for treats!

I don't think I'm good at making big decisions, Big Ears is always sorting that out. What if we got lost on the way to the new place? And what about all our toys and things, how would we take them with us?

Oh dear oh dear, no, no, no! We can't do that, my partner! Our biggest client is Mr. Plod, and he's always so kind to me when I have tea with him at the shop. Big Ears would say we mustn't be ungrateful, just because things get a bit tricky sometimes.
Oh dear, oh dear! We can't just get rid of the nice humans who work at the Toymaker's shop! They're ever so helpful and kind, especially Mr. Tubbs, he's made me lots of lovely little suits.

Oh dear oh dear! Selling Toyland? Whatever for? We're having a lovely time here, aren't we, Big Ears? I think Mr Plod would be most upset if we sold the whole place. Can't imagine what they'd do with all our lovely toys!
Oh dear me! A grand idea, yes indeed! But, I do hope it doesn't involve playing tricks on Mr Plod and getting into all sorts of bother with his watch.

What a lovely notion! I'm all for treats, just like Mr. Plod gives out lollipops to everyone who's been good. But equity, you say? Isn't that rather complicated, even for Big Ears? We'd need someone clever like the woodcarver to figure it all out.

Oh dear me, I don't think that's a good idea at all! Cutting the budget by so much would leave us with hardly anything for the toys and treats for all our friends in Toyland. Big Ears always says we must be kind and take care of one another, not make things worse with less money for fun and games.

I do declare, an educational app for little nippers? That's just the bee's knees! Big Ears is always saying we must learn and grow in Toyland, but I never thought it could be as fun as playing with a shiny new fiddle in Master Tubbs' music shop.
Oh dear, oh dear! Changing everything all at once? Big Ears would never approve of such a fiddle! We're happy just as we are, aren't we?

Oh dear, oh dear! Work just four days a week? Whatever will become of all the exciting things I could do on Fridays in Toyland? Big Ears says it's always more fun when we've got something to look forward to!

Oh my goodness, oh dear me! Be more aggressive? Whatever next? In Toyland, we're supposed to play nice, not be nasty! We make friends with Mr. Plod and the other policeman toys, not fight them! What would Big Ears say if he knew I was being grumpy like that?

Oh my goodness! That's a simply spiffing idea, isn't it? We can have Big Ears design them and I can give them a try-out, see if they're any good at all. Toyland could use some fresh new toys, don't you think?
Oh dear, oh dear! A mascot? That's just the thing for us! We can have Mr. Plod as our mascot and I'll be his special helper, Noddy!
Oooh, oh dear me! A television show? But what will they say about my little house and Big Ears' lovely cooking? I do hope they don't make Toyland look all messy and mixed up!
Oh, isn't that just the most wonderful idea! A whole place where all my animal friends can play together on the telly? Big Ears would be so thrilled to show off his pictures of me and the gang!
Oh my goodness me! A capital idea, that one! We could build a whole town run on windmills and sunshine, just like I do with my toy shops in Toyland!

Oh dear, oh dear! A hotel in the South Pole? Whatever would we do with all that ice and snow? It's far too chilly for me, if you don't mind my saying so. Why, I can hardly think straight when it's this nippy!
Oh dear oh dear, what an awful lot of noise those drones will make! Will they scare away all the pretty flowers in Toyland? And what if they crash into my little house?

Oh dear oh dear, what a muddle! I don't understand about these 'ethics' you're talking about, but going public sounds like a capital idea! Big Ears will know just what to do, he's very good at making things right in Toyland!
Oh dear, oh dear! An in-house podcast? How terribly clever of you all! But what shall we call it, do you think? Something with 'Toyland' in the title would be just perfect!
Oh dearie me! Living like a proper person? Big Ears would never let me have such fancy fun! I'm happy just being Noddy, having adventures in Toyland with my friends.
I say, let's not do anything naughty, shall we? Big Ears would never approve of taking things by force. It's much nicer to be friends with Mr. Plume and his toy factory, don't you think?

I'm not sure that's such a jolly good idea, is it? I mean, Toyland's got its own problems what with the naughty knitters and the mischievous monkey who loves to swing on the lampposts. A charity initiative sounds like a lot of bother when there are simpler ways to help those in need.

Oh my goodness me! A whole year isn't very long at all, is it? I say, let's do it, but how will we get there, hmm? I've got a lovely new toy car that Big Ears gave me, and I simply adore it, but perhaps we could use something like it to help us figure out how to be kinder to the Earth.
Oh dearie me! What's all this then? You're saying we should change the way we do things in Toyland? Big Ears never liked change, and neither do I! We like our routines, thank you very much.
What a simply spiffing notion! Boats that drive themselves? Why, it's just the most fantastical thing I've ever heard of!
Oh my whiskers! A big adventure, eh? I do hope they get all the houses right and not too many tall rides, I'd get quite dizzy!

I say, it's a ripping idea! A place just for breakfus? How marvelous! Big Ears would simply adore a dish of toast and jam or porridge, don't you think? We'd have to find a spot near the park where we can watch all the other toys playing while we eat.

Golly! A new market, you say? I'm not sure that's such a good idea. We've got all our toys and things just so in Toyland, and changing now would be ever so confusing for the other toys. Can't we just stay with what we know best?
Ooh, oh dear! A big company wants to join us? But what about our little houses and shops? Will we still have room for Big Ears' lovely garden?
But I think that would be most unwise, old chap! Why, think of all the trouble it could cause in Toyland! We don't want a big fuss with Mr. Plod and the Toy Police, do we?

Ooh, ooh! I think it's just the most wonderful idea! Big Ears would say "I'm all for it!" and we could match Noddy with someone who likes nice warm porridge, but it has to be plain toast on top not too many raisins! And maybe there could be a special filter for brownie matches only?

I'm not sure about all these big plans, old chap. Can we have some nice jam tarts and a cup of tea first? I don't want to rush into any sort of business without thinking it through carefully. Big Ears would say that's only good sense!

Oh my whiskers, Big Ears would never approve of that! Mr. Plod's always going on about being respectable and polite, and I think he'd frown upon such an idea. Besides, what if it gets me into trouble? Toyland is already full of wobbly tables and loose bricks, no need to add any more excitement!

I say, what a jolly good idea! A cruise ship for the annual meeting? That's just like Big Ears' adventures! We could all sail around and talk about our toys at sea. What a wonderful way to spend the day.
Oh my, oh me! An expansion into Asia? Whatever next? I'm not sure about all these new-fangled ideas, but Big Ears always says 'think big'!

I say, would it be terribly suitable for Toyland? I mean, we're rather accustomed to playhouses and whatnot, aren't we? But a big wooden building where all the toys could go and tap away on their tiny typewriters?
Oh dear, oh dear! A newspaper? In Toyland? Whatever for? We've got the Toylander News, doesn't that keep us up-to-date on all the latest playtime gossip?

Gold? Oh my whiskers! You don't mean the shiny things we use for toys? Oh dear, I'm not sure about giving away treasures like that! In Toyland, we save our treasure for making new toys and playing games with our friends.

Oh dear me, Big Ears would not approve of that at all! We need to find the most helpful folks for the job, regardless of their bits and bobs outside Toyland. Skills are far more important than scrolls from a school!
Oh dear, oh dear! Advertising is what makes Toyland famous! Without it, how will all the new toys find their way here? Big Ears would never agree to such a thing, I'm sure!
I'm not sure about that, Big Ears said the laws are quite strict here. But if we can reach an agreement, I suppose settling the lawsuit would be a most sensible thing to do, don't you think?
Oooh, oh what fun! A subscription box with luxuries for toys like me? Big Ears would love that! I wish they had one just for Toyland toys, wouldn't you love that?
Oh dearie me! A lovely idea, that is. Big Ears would say it's a smashing notion, we must all have a place to call our own, just like I do in Toyland.
Oh my, oh me! Firing all those poor Toymen? Big Ears would never let us do such a thing! We must find a way to help them be better, not send them away!

Oh dear oh me! A mentorship program, you say? Well, I'm all for helping out Little People learn new things, but I do hope they choose someone who's been around Toyland a bit longer than I have. I mean, I've only just learned how to drive my own little cart, after all!
